``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Courts must specify a “minimum period of confinement” and may add a subsequent “indeterminate period” of the sentence. Courts have broad discretion over minimum terms, maximum sentences, and the ratio between the two.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population increased 20%, changing from 7,431 in 2010 to 8,907 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 234%, dropping from 1,695 in 2010 to 5,659 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 0% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 0% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and unknown people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age distribution data is incomplete or missing. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Idaho. The largest change was for murder and non-negligent manslaughter offenses, which NA by NaN%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Idaho, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 5 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 1.5 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.4 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
